subject: Chemistry
title: Porcelain: The White Gold
description: Learn Porcelain: The White Gold in one minute.

hook.text: Europeans called Chinese porcelain 'white gold' and spent centuries trying to copy it. An imprisoned alchemist cracked it.

buildup.text: China guarded the secret for a thousand years: kaolin clay fired at 1,400°C to create translucent ceramic.

discovery.text: In 1708, Johann Böttger, imprisoned in Saxony to make gold, found that kaolin clay plus feldspar made true porcelain.

twist.text: Augustus the Strong kept Böttger locked up for years afterward—porcelain was more profitable than gold.

climax.text: Meissen porcelain became Europe's most valuable export. The alchemist who failed at gold created a billion-dollar industry.

punchline.text: Imprisoned to make gold, he made porcelain instead. Failure became fortune.

quiz.question: Who was the first European to successfully produce true porcelain?
options: Johann Böttger, an alchemist imprisoned to make gold
Leonardo da Vinci during his experiments in Milan
Robert Boyle in his Oxford laboratory
